column2: A Windows user says the computer is slow right now. Which built-in tool should you open first to quickly view current CPU, memory, disk, and network activity?
column5: Task Manager is the fast local view for current resource pressure. Event Viewer is better for historical logs, and Performance Monitor is better for longer-term collection.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 7
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Performance Source::Messer-v140

column2: In Task Manager, which area lets a technician quickly start, stop, or restart background services?
column5: This is a quick service-control view inside Task Manager. Dedicated service management may offer more detail, but the objective specifically includes services under Task Manager.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 7
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Services Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user complains that many applications open automatically after sign-in. Which Task Manager area should you use to disable unnecessary sign-in startup apps?
column5: Startup focuses on programs that launch when the user logs in. Processes shows what is running now, but Startup controls future sign-in behavior.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 7
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Startup Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Task Manager area should you use to sort running applications and background processes by resource usage?
column5: Processes is the quick view for what is running now. It can combine apps, background processes, and services into a sortable view.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 7
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Processes Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Task Manager area shows connected users and can provide options such as disconnecting a user?
column5: Use this when the scenario is about logged-in users or user sessions, not hardware drivers, event logs, or disk layout.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 7
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Users Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to review yesterday's application crash and related Windows log entries. Which MMC snap-in should they use?
column5: Event Viewer is for consolidated historical logs such as application, security, setup, and system events. It is not the best tool for live performance measurement.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Logs Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool should you use to view a graphical disk and volume layout before creating, deleting, or changing partitions?
column5: Disk Management can change disk structures and erase data. Verify the selected disk and backup status before making destructive storage changes.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Storage Source::Messer-v140

column2: A maintenance script needs to run automatically every Friday evening. Which Windows tool should you use?
column5: Task Scheduler is the built-in tool for scheduled or triggered applications and scripts. Task Manager shows running work; it does not create recurring jobs.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Automation Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows device has a driver problem after a hardware change. Which tool is the best place to inspect the device status and manage the driver?
column5: Device Manager is the Windows hardware and driver management tool. It is the right match when the support task points to hardware recognition or hardware-specific driver compatibility.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Drivers Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool is used to view and manage user and trusted certificates?
column5: Certificate Manager is the objective-listed tool for certificate stores. Do not confuse it with Device Manager, which manages hardware devices and drivers.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Certificates Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows MMC snap-in should you use to manage local user accounts and local group membership?
column5: Use Local Users and Groups for local accounts and group membership. Domain user management belongs to directory services tools outside this objective's local tool focus.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Users Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool is the better choice for collecting long-term CPU, memory, and disk statistics with alerts and reports: Task Manager or Performance Monitor?
column5: Task Manager is quick and immediate. Performance Monitor is built for longer collection, stored statistics, alerts, automated actions, and reports.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Performance Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool should you use to configure local policy settings on a standalone Windows system?
column5: gpedit.msc manages local policy on a single device. In Active Directory environments, Group Policy can also be managed centrally with domain tools.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Policy Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool provides a broad system overview of hardware resources, components, and the software environment?
column5: System Information is useful for inventory and configuration review. It is not primarily a live performance troubleshooting view.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Inventory Source::Messer-v140

column2: Task Manager shows high disk activity, but you need a more detailed live view by CPU, memory, disk, and network categories. Which tool should you open?
column5: Resource Monitor provides a deeper real-time breakdown than Task Manager. It is not the same as Performance Monitor, which is better for longer-term collection and reports.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Performance Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool centralizes boot, startup, and service configuration for controlled startup troubleshooting?
column5: System Configuration can change how Windows boots and which startup items or services are active. That makes it useful but also potentially disruptive.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Startup Source::Messer-v140

column2: A Windows computer is low on space and you want a built-in tool to remove unused or unnecessary files. Which tool should you use?
column5: Disk Cleanup is for freeing space by selecting categories of unneeded files. It does not reorganize file fragments or manage partitions.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Storage Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows tool is used to defragment a traditional hard drive, and why is it not a routine SSD maintenance task?
column4: Disk Defragment. SSDs do not need traditional file-fragment defragmentation.
column5: The objective lists Disk Defragment as a Windows tool. Messer notes the SSD distinction, which helps prevent applying hard-drive maintenance habits to SSDs.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows Storage Source::Messer-v140

column2: A support procedure requires changing a Windows Registry value. Which tool is used, and what precaution should you take first?
column4: Use Registry Editor and back up the registry or the relevant keys before editing.
column5: The registry is a central configuration database. Registry edits can affect Windows, services, drivers, applications, and user interface behavior.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 9
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield Windows Registry Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is the purpose of the Microsoft Management Console in Windows administration?
column4: It provides a framework for loading administrative snap-ins such as Event Viewer, Disk Management, Task Scheduler, and Local Users and Groups.
column5: The console itself is a container. In support scenarios, the specific snap-in is usually the important tool selection.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.4; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 8
column10: A+::220-1202::1.4 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsFeaturesandTools Basic Windows MMC Source::Messer-v140
